title: achillea millefolium subsp. collina
intro: Achillea millefolium subsp. collina, also known as mountain yarrow, is a subspecies of the yarrow plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, digestive issues, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Achillea millefolium subsp. collina include reducing fever, relieving headaches and muscle pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions, such as skin irritations and respiratory issues.
